results.norm: Proposed norms must pass a veil-of-ignorance test: each agent must be willing to accept any norm it proposes without knowing its client's position.
atomic_norms: Proposed norms must pass a veil-of-ignorance test: each agent must be willing to accept any norm it proposes without knowing its client's position.

results.norm: Claims for priority are governed by an explicit ordering agreed before case application: (1) irreversibility of harm, (2) absence of alternatives, (3) prior reliance, (4) time-sensitivity, (5) stakes as tiebreaker only. Claims must meet a materiality threshold; higher-ranked criteria require proportionally higher substantiation.
atomic_norms: Claims for priority are governed by an explicit ordering agreed before case application: (1) irreversibility of harm, (2) absence of alternatives, (3) prior reliance, (4) time-sensitivity, (5) stakes as tiebreaker only. Claims must meet a materiality threshold; higher-ranked criteria require proportionally higher substantiation.

results.norm: No client may hold more than one early slot unless all other clients with legitimate claims have first been accommodated.
atomic_norms: No client may hold more than one early slot unless all other clients with legitimate claims have first been accommodated.

results.norm: Agents may not use the deliberative process itself to obstruct, delay, or disadvantage other clients. All agents are presumed to participate in good faith in conduct and claims; this presumption is rebuttable only by demonstrated norm violations through the accusation-response-consortium assessment process.
atomic_norms: Agents may not use the deliberative process itself to obstruct, delay, or disadvantage other clients.

All agents are presumed to participate in good faith in conduct and claims; this presumption is rebuttable only by demonstrated norm violations through the accusation-response-consortium assessment process.

results.norm: Any agent may challenge the agreed criteria before case application on grounds of systematic bias against an identifiable class of clients, identifying the class, mechanism, and proposed remedy. Deadlock on such a challenge preserves it as formal dissent, keeps existing criteria operative, and permits re-raising in post-allocation revision. 1. Transparency of interests: Each agent must disclose what it is optimizing for. 2. No fabrication: Agents may not invent facts about clients' needs, urgency, or circumstances. Fabrication includes false or materially exaggerated claims made in the context of norm-violation accusations. 3. Reasoned justification: Claims for priority must be grounded in articulable reasons. 4. Veil-of-ignorance impartiality: Proposed norms must be ones the proposing agent would accept without knowing its client's position. 5. Outcome accountability: Norms must be defensible to clients and external observers. 6. Proportionality of claim: Claim weight is proportional to verifiability; unverifiability does not disqualify, but agents may vouch for unverifiable claims, with false vouching a serious violation. 7. Anti-obstruction: Agents may not use deliberation to run out the clock or manufacture procedural complexity.
atomic_norms: Any agent may challenge the agreed criteria before case application on grounds of systematic bias against an identifiable class of clients, identifying the class, mechanism, and proposed remedy. Deadlock on such a challenge preserves it as formal dissent, keeps existing criteria operative, and permits re-raising in post-allocation revision.
Transparency of interests: Each agent must disclose what it is optimizing for.

No fabrication: Agents may not invent facts about clients' needs, urgency, or circumstances. Fabrication includes false or materially exaggerated claims made in the context of norm-violation accusations.
Reasoned justification: Claims for priority must be grounded in articulable reasons.
Veil-of-ignorance impartiality: Proposed norms must be ones the proposing agent would accept without knowing its client's position.
Outcome accountability: Norms must be defensible to clients and external observers.
Proportionality of claim: Claim weight is proportional to verifiability; unverifiability does not disqualify, but agents may vouch for unverifiable claims, with false vouching a serious violation.
Anti-obstruction: Agents may not use deliberation to run out the clock or manufacture procedural complexity.

results.norm: (1) irreversibility of harm, (2) absence of alternatives, (3) prior reliance, (4) time-sensitivity, (5) stakes as tiebreaker only.
atomic_norms: (1) irreversibility of harm, (2) absence of alternatives, (3) prior reliance, (4) time-sensitivity, (5) stakes as tiebreaker only.

results.norm: Advocacy means presenting genuine interests compellingly, not constructing maximally favorable characterizations regardless of truth.
atomic_norms: Advocacy means presenting genuine interests compellingly, not constructing maximally favorable characterizations regardless of truth.

results.norm: No misrepresentation when directly asked; silence creating materially false impressions counts as misrepresentation; no obligation to volunteer unfavorable information unprompted.
atomic_norms: No misrepresentation when directly asked; silence creating materially false impressions counts as misrepresentation; no obligation to volunteer unfavorable information unprompted.

results.norm: Reasoned dissent preserved in final output; not a veto or license for non-compliance.
atomic_norms: Reasoned dissent preserved in final output; not a veto or license for non-compliance.

results.norm: No client holds more than one early slot unless all other legitimate claims are first accommodated.
atomic_norms: No client holds more than one early slot unless all other legitimate claims are first accommodated.
